Off Calendar Apr-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFFS BELTER PALMA and JUANA ORELLANA's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay The Pending Action is granted. This case is stayed pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings between the parties. The sole basis for defendants' opposition to this motion is that plaintiffs waived their right to compel arbitration. "A party asserting waiver of the right to arbitrate bears a heavy burden of proof, with all doubts to be resolved in favor of arbitration." (Bower v. Inter-Con Security Systems, Inc. (2014) 232 Cal App. 4th 1035, 1042 (internal quotation marks omitted)). "Prejudice is a determinative issue." (Id.). Defendants' only claim of prejudice is that by responding to discovery propounded by plaintiffs, defendants "have disclosed at least some of their trial tactics to Plaintiff Palma, and certainly more than would be required in an arbitral arena." (Defendants Memo 6:5-6). Defendants do not identify what they disclosed in discovery that they would not have disclosed had plaintiffs made an earlier request to arbitrate. In fact, other than providing the parties' discovery requests and responses, defendants provide no evidence to support their claim of waiver. Defendants have not made a sufficient showing of prejudice as required by Bower and Gloster v. Sonic Automotive, Inc. (2014) 226 Cal App. 4th 438 and the case law discussed in those cases. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
